title: Back-of-the-Envelope Sanity Check
description: A junior engineer asks if the new feature can really handle launch-day traffic. Walk through a quick estimate.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Marketing says we'll get a million users at launch. Can our system handle it?

templates.text: Let's do a quick back-of-the-envelope check before we panic.
templates.hint_zh: 我們先做個粗估,別先慌。

templates.text: If a million users hit us in one hour, that's about three hundred requests per second on average.
templates.hint_zh: 一百萬用戶集中在一小時,平均約每秒三百個請求。

templates.text: One node handles maybe two hundred requests per second. So we need at least two, three with margin.
templates.hint_zh: 一個節點大概每秒兩百請求。所以至少兩台,留 margin 抓三台。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: What if traffic spikes ten times higher in the first minute?

templates.text: Then we need a queue or a CDN cache. Otherwise we'll see timeouts.
templates.hint_zh: 那要加 queue 或 CDN cache,不然會 timeout。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Should I add Redis as a cache layer?

templates.text: Yes, for the read-heavy paths. Most launch traffic reads the same five things anyway.
templates.hint_zh: 讀為主的路徑加。launch 大部分流量也只是讀那五個東西。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Cool. I'll write up the estimate and the plan.

templates.text: Good. Numbers in the doc, not in your head.
templates.hint_zh: 好,把數字寫進文件,不要只放在腦袋裡。
